description_long: This object is a whole coconut with its fibrous, tan-brown husk intact, featuring a rough, irregular surface covered in dense, interwoven fibers. The top of the coconut has been cut open, revealing a grayish, polygonal opening that exposes the interior, and a plastic drinking straw is inserted into the opening, suggesting it is prepared for drinking coconut water. The straw is white and slightly bent, protruding from the top at an angle in some views. The coconut's body is nearly spherical but slightly flattened at the top and bottom, with subtle variations in the density and direction of the surface fibers. The coloration is a mix of light and medium browns, with some areas appearing more densely textured than others. No additional objects or environmental context are visible around the coconut.
description: A whole, fibrous brown coconut with a cut opening at the top and a white drinking straw inserted, showing a rough, natural texture.
description_view_0: The coconut is seen from the front with the straw pointing straight up and the cut opening clearly visible at the top.
description_view_1: The coconut is rotated to show the straw bent at an angle, emphasizing the opening and the straw's insertion point.
description_view_2: The coconut is viewed with the straw vertical and the opening centered, highlighting the symmetrical shape and dense fiber texture.
description_view_3: The coconut is rotated further, showing the straw at a more pronounced angle and the fibrous surface wrapping around the body.
attribute_husk_pattern: The coconut's outer husk displays a dense, tangled network of coarse, light to medium brown fibers running in various directions, with some areas showing more concentrated fiber clusters and others appearing slightly smoother. The fibers are mostly short and randomly oriented, creating a rough, irregular pattern with visible striations and subtle color variations.
